productApiFindings.lag0Blocker: DistilledMlpPredictionModel.g.cs defines public const float LagCompensationPixels = 0.5f and private ApplyLagCompensation mutates dx/dy after quantization. DwmAwareCursorPositionPredictor exposes offset/model/gain/horizon settings but no runtime lag setting; reflection cannot change a const in already compiled generated code.

DwmAwareCursorPositionPredictor exposes offset/model/gain/horizon settings but no runtime lag setting; reflection cannot change a const in already compiled generated code.

csharp_lag0_offsetm4ms.reason: DistilledMlpPredictionModel.g.cs defines public const float LagCompensationPixels = 0.5f and private ApplyLagCompensation mutates dx/dy after quantization. DwmAwareCursorPositionPredictor exposes offset/model/gain/horizon settings but no runtime lag setting; reflection cannot change a const in already compiled generated code.

interpretation.minus4Evidence: Direct C# chronological replay through DwmAwareCursorPositionPredictor also favors lag0.5 offset -4ms over offset 0ms and -2ms on all p95, stop p95, post-stop jitter, and high-speed p95. Replay fidelity is medium because controller reset/session boundaries beyond predictor idle gaps are not fully captured. Caveat: the C# direct stop-overshoot tail is worse at -4ms, so visible tail frames must be inspected before unconditional adoption.
interpretation.lag0Evidence: lag0 at offset 0 reduces overshoot and jitter versus lag0.5 offset 0, but worsens stop p95 and highSpeed in Step 7. Also it is not product-switchable without regenerating the generated DistilledMLP source.
interpretation.visualRisk: Offset -4 ms reduces the visible acute deceleration overshoot risk in POC reference metrics, but must be replayed through product chronological timing before product adoption.
interpretation.safeImmediateProductChange: Offset -4ms can move forward as a product candidate behind a guarded setting/flag or controlled validation build, not as an unconditional default. lag0 still requires generated model/runtime shape work before product evaluation.

conclusion.text: C# chronological replay completed with medium fidelity and supports target offset -4ms as the next guarded product candidate. Direct C# p95/jitter/high-speed metrics improve strongly, but stop-overshoot tail worsens and needs visual inspection. Do not treat lag0 as directly validated because current generated DistilledMLP source bakes lag0.5 as const.

nextSteps: Run the same harness after adding explicit controller reset/session markers to raise fidelity to high.
If lag0 must be tested in C#, generate a POC-only DistilledMlpPredictionModel variant with LagCompensationPixels = 0.0f or add a runtime/generated lag selector.
Consider a small product validation build that sets DwmPredictionTargetOffsetMilliseconds = -4 for DistilledMLP only, with rollback to current offset.

harness.fidelityNotes: Uses runtimeSchedulerPoll rows from trace.csv as chronological calls, CursorPollSample.Position from cursorX/cursorY, runtimeSchedulerSampleRecordedTicks as sample timestamp, predictionTargetTicks/dwmQpcRefreshPeriod as scheduler timing. Controller-level reset boundaries beyond idle gaps are not present.
harness.lag0Blocker: DistilledMlpPredictionModel.g.cs exposes LagCompensationPixels as public const float 0.5f and ApplyLagCompensation is private; DwmAwareCursorPositionPredictor has public offset/gain/model settings but no runtime lag setting.
